title: Smoky Middle Eastern Tahini Pilaf One-Pot
description: A halal-friendly, one-pot dish from Saudi Arabia that combines smoky flavors with creamy tahini and a glassy-jelly texture.
instructions: In a lazy Susan spinner or large saucepan with a heat diffuser, toast the pumpkin seeds over medium heat until fragrant.
Add the rice and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes.
Add the chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 18 minutes or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
While the rice cooks, whisk together the tahini, miso paste, and 2 tablespoons of water in a small bowl until smooth.
After 12 minutes of cooking the rice, stir in the tahini mixture and continue to cook, uncovered, for an additional 4 minutes or until the mixture has formed a glassy-jelly texture.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
